body: {"rationale":"Title (imperative, names subsystem + change), description (PROBLEM / ROOT CAUSE WITH file:line / PROPOSED FIX / NON-SCOPE), labels (phase, area×2, kind, cross-refs adr:023 + spec:FEAT-011), parent + deps, and cold self-test all pass. AC is numbered and each item is independently verifiable. Two criteria fail: (1) No AC item names a Test* function or a `go test -run` filter — `ddx skills check` (AC 8) and `make skill-schema` (AC 9) are not Go test expressions and do not satisfy this requirement. (2) The penultimate AC line must be `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./\u003cpaths\u003e/... green`; instead AC 9 is `make skill-schema`, violating the required terminal-two-line pattern (go test, then lefthook). Because kind:doc beads often have no changed Go source, no existing *_test.go may be directly relevant, but the rubric does not auto-waive this criterion — the bead must either name a covering test or carry an explicit waiver label.","score":6,"suggested_fixes":["Add an AC item: `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/skills/... green` (the skills package exercises skill-file loading and would catch regressions even if no new Go code is added). If truly no Go test covers this path, state that explicitly in the AC and add a waiver label such as `waiver:no-go-test` so reviewers can approve consciously.","Reorder the final AC items so the second-to-last line is the `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test` line and the last line is `lefthook run pre-commit passes`. Currently AC 9 is `make skill-schema` and AC 10 is lefthook, which puts a non-go-test gate in the penultimate slot."],"waivers_applied":[]}

{"rationale":"Criteria 1 (title), 2 (root cause with file:line), 6 (labels with cross-refs), 7 (parent+deps), and 8 (standalone description) all pass cleanly. Criterion 4 (wired-in assertion) is marginally satisfied by AC#8 (ddx skills check). Criteria 3 and 5 fail: AC contains no Test* function name or `go test -run` filter, and the second-to-last AC line is `make skill-schema` rather than `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./\u003cpaths\u003e/... green`. For a kind:doc bead whose NON-SCOPE explicitly excludes Go changes, criteria 3 and 5 are waiver-eligible, but waivers must be declared explicitly in the bead rather than inferred.","score":6,"suggested_fixes":["Add an explicit waiver declaration to the AC block, e.g.: 'NOTE: kind:doc bead with no Go source changes; go test criterion waived per bead-authoring-template §5.' This makes the omission intentional rather than an oversight.","If any Go file is touched during implementation (e.g., cli/internal/skills/ embedded copies), add: '9. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/skills/... green`' before the lefthook line and name the relevant Test* function or `go test -run` pattern.","AC item 8 ('ddx skills check …') is the closest thing to a wired-in assertion but it is a CLI invocation, not a test harness call. If a unit test exists for skill validation (e.g., TestSkillCheck), reference it by name to satisfy criterion 3."],"waivers_applied":[]}

rationale: bead: ddx-b14c4924
title: skills: update bead-lifecycle readiness contract

All 10 acceptance criteria are already satisfied by the current repository state.
The work was completed in commit cd652fb59 ("docs(skills): sync lifecycle vocabulary sources").

Evidence per AC:

AC 1: skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md line 16 — "MODE: readiness" listed under invocation modes.
AC 2: skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md line 30 — "`MODE: intake` is the compatibility name for readiness mode."
AC 3: skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md lines 50-69 — all 8 ADR-023 reason codes present:
       too_large, ambiguous_scope, missing_root_cause_or_current_state, missing_verification,
       missing_code_path_assertion, missing_dependency_or_parent, hidden_external_blocker,
       already_satisfied_candidate.
AC 4: skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md lines 41-46 — infra failures (provider outages, quota,
       transport, git index locks, etc.) must be reported as system readiness failures, not bead defects.
AC 5: skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md line 31 — "Treat it exactly like `MODE: readiness` unless
       the caller gives a narrower schema." The preclaim_intake_hook.go prompt includes explicit schema
       instructions that constitute the narrower schema, so the legacy intake contract is preserved.
AC 6: skills/ddx/SKILL.md line 107 — "assess bead readiness" → bead-lifecycle/ in intent router.
AC 7: diff across all four locations produces no output (files identical).
AC 8: `ddx skills check` returns "validated 8 skill files" with exit 0.
AC 9: `make skill-schema` (cd cli/) returns "validated 80 skill files" with exit 0.
AC 10: `lefthook run pre-commit` completes with no failures.
